rationale: The citing paper explicitly frames its contribution as extending prior promotion-versus-prevention persuasion effects by introducing processing-mode boundary conditions, and the cited paper directly studies regulatory focus fit with an involvement (low vs. high processing) boundary and selective-processing mechanism. Given this strong construct/mechanism overlap (regulatory focus, promotion/prevention appeals, resource-dependent processing), this is plausibly a substantive theory/mechanism source rather than an incidental mention, despite only DOI-level matched reference evidence.
